title: Duplicate MTU constants may drift: LICHEN_LORA_MTU vs LICHEN_L2_MTU
description:
Locations:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.h line 54: LICHEN_LORA_MTU = 200
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.h line 45: LICHEN_L2_MTU = 200

These constants are currently identical but represent different abstraction layers:
- LICHEN_LORA_MTU: LoRa-level effective payload (after compression, before framing)
- LICHEN_L2_MTU: IPv6-level MTU exposed to Zephyr net stack

What breaks: If a contributor changes one without the other, the layers will have mismatched expectations. The L2 layer uses LICHEN_L2_MTU for buffer sizing, but LICHEN_LORA_MTU documents the actual LoRa constraint. A change to the LoRa framing overhead would require updating LICHEN_LORA_MTU, but LICHEN_L2_MTU (which is derived from it) might be forgotten.

Suggested fix: Either:
1. Make LICHEN_L2_MTU derive from LICHEN_LORA_MTU (e.g., `#define LICHEN_L2_MTU LICHEN_LORA_MTU`)
2. Add a comment cross-referencing both constants
3. Add a static_assert ensuring they remain equal if that's the intent
close_reason: Made LICHEN_L2_MTU derive from LICHEN_LORA_MTU via include and macro redefinition. This eliminates the possibility of drift.
